title: FundedPrime Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: FundedPrime Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). FundedPrime (4.0★, $35/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using FundedPrime — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is FundedPrime?

FundedPrime is an Australian prop firm backed by PropTradeTech that gives traders access to funded trading accounts across forex, stocks, commodities, indices, and crypto. Founded in 2023, it powers accounts through Eightcap, a regulated Australian broker offering 800+ tradeable instruments. Unlike traditional prop firms, FundedPrime specializes in multiple challenge types—including a unique Meme Coin challenge—with an 80% profit split and entry fees starting at just $35/month for the Stock Challenge. With a 4.0/5 rating, it's gaining traction among budget-conscious traders and crypto enthusiasts who want flexible evaluation structures without brutal time pressure.

How to Create Your FundedPrime Account

Getting started with FundedPrime takes roughly 10–15 minutes. Here's the process:

  • Visit FundedPrime.com: Navigate to their signup page and click "Create Account."
  • Provide basic information: Enter your email address, full name, and create a secure password. FundedPrime will send a verification email—check your inbox and spam folder.
  • Confirm your email: Click the verification link to activate your account.
  • Complete profile details: Fill in your phone number, country of residence, and trading experience level. FundedPrime asks this to ensure compliance with Australian regulations.
  • Identity verification (KYC): You'll need to upload a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID) and proof of address (utility bill, bank statement dated within 90 days). This typically takes 1–24 hours to verify.
  • Review terms and conditions: Accept FundedPrime's trading rules, profit-split terms, and challenge withdrawal requirements before proceeding.
  • Choose your challenge: You don't select a challenge during signup—instead, you do this after verification when your account is live.

No funding is required upfront. You'll only pay the challenge fee ($35–$67 depending on the challenge type) when you enroll in a specific evaluation.

Setting Up FundedPrime for the First Time

Once your account is verified and active, you'll land on the FundedPrime dashboard. Here's what you'll see and how to configure it:

  • Dashboard Home: The main screen shows your account balance, active challenges (if any), profit/loss for the current evaluation, and quick access to trading tools. Familiarize yourself with the layout before diving in.
  • Select Your Challenge: Click "Start Challenge" or "New Challenge" to browse the four challenge types. Read each carefully—the Stock Challenge ($35/mo) is cheapest but focuses only on stocks, while the 1-Phase Challenge ($55/mo) and 2-Phase Challenge ($45/mo) give you access to forex and other markets. The Meme Coin Challenge ($67/mo) is FundedPrime's unique offering if you trade altcoins.
  • Connect to Eightcap Broker: FundedPrime uses Eightcap as its underlying broker. Your challenge account automatically connects to Eightcap's trading platform—you don't manually integrate anything. Download the Eightcap MT4, MT5, or Eightcap web terminal depending on your preference.
  • Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): Go to Account Settings > Security and enable 2FA via authenticator app or SMS. This protects your funded account from unauthorized access.
  • Set Withdrawal Preferences: Under Account > Payouts, choose your withdrawal method. FundedPrime processes payouts to bank transfer or cryptocurrency. Be aware: bank transfers incur a $50 flat fee, so plan accordingly when withdrawing profits.
  • Adjust Notification Settings: In Settings > Notifications, enable alerts for profit milestones, rule violations, or account balance changes. FundedPrime's alert system notifies you via email or in-app when you hit drawdown limits or complete phase requirements.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Alerts & Notifications
FundedPrime sends real-time alerts when you're approaching drawdown limits, hitting profit targets, or violating trading rules (like news trading restrictions). These arrive via email and in-app notifications, giving you instant visibility into your account health.

2. Mobile App Trading
Trade on the go using the Eightcap mobile app (iOS and Android). The app syncs with your funded account, showing live P&L, open positions, and account balance. For swing traders and longer-term strategies, the mobile app is essential—you're not tethered to a desktop.

3. Broker Integration with Eightcap
FundedPrime's backbone is Eightcap, a regulated Australian broker. This integration means you get access to 800+ instruments—forex pairs, US stocks, commodities like gold and oil, indices such as the S&P 500, and cryptocurrencies. All trading occurs within Eightcap's infrastructure, so execution quality, spreads, and instrument availability depend on Eightcap's platform.

4. Performance Analytics
The Dashboard displays detailed analytics: daily, weekly, and monthly P&L charts; win rate percentage; profit factor; and maximum drawdown tracking. These metrics update in real-time, allowing you to assess your trading performance throughout the evaluation period. Export reports for personal analysis.

5. Risk Management Rules
FundedPrime enforces strict risk management via built-in limits. Each challenge has specific drawdown caps (usually 5–10% of the account) and daily loss limits. Exceed these, and you're disqualified—no second chances on most tiers. The platform prevents you from opening trades that would violate these rules, so you can't accidentally blow the account.

6. Challenge Rules Engine
FundedPrime automates compliance checks. The system flags news trading within 10 minutes of major economic events (on most challenge types), restricts certain strategies on specific instruments, and monitors scalping behavior. You'll receive warnings before a rule violation, giving you time to adjust.

7. Multi-Challenge Management
If you're juggling multiple challenges (e.g., a Stock Challenge and a 1-Phase Challenge simultaneously), the dashboard lets you switch between accounts with a single click. You can monitor all active challenges without logging out.

FundedPrime Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Stock Challenge — $35/month (Best for Beginners)
Smallest entry fee, limited to stocks listed on major exchanges. Account size is typically $5,000. The 80% profit split applies. This tier is ideal if you're new to prop trading and want to test the waters cheaply. Drawdown limits are usually 5% daily and 10% maximum.

2-Phase Challenge — $45/month (Best for Intermediate Traders)
This is FundedPrime's sweet spot for most traders. Phase 1 requires hitting a 10% profit target with 5% daily/10% max drawdown caps. Phase 2 increases the target but removes the daily limit (only max drawdown remains). Unlocks forex, indices, and commodities. Account sizes vary but typically start at $10,000.

1-Phase Challenge — $55/month (Best for Advanced Traders)
Single evaluation phase—hit the profit target (usually 8–10%) and you're funded. No daily drawdown cap, only a max drawdown threshold (typically 10%). Fastest path to a live account if you're confident. Includes all markets: forex, stocks, crypto, commodities, indices.

Meme Coin Challenge — $67/month (Best for Crypto Traders)
FundedPrime's unique offering. This challenge focuses exclusively on meme coins and altcoins on major exchanges. No other prop firm offers this. If you specialize in crypto volatility trading, this is your lane. Same challenge structure as others (phases, drawdown limits, 80% split), but the instrument universe is crypto-only.

Note: All challenge fees are non-refundable. If you don't pass the evaluation, you've lost the entry fee. Plan accordingly.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of FundedPrime

1. Use the Stock Challenge as a Dry Run
Before committing to a $45+ challenge, spend one month on the $35 Stock Challenge to familiarize yourself with FundedPrime's rules engine, drawdown enforcement, and alert system. The muscle memory pays dividends on higher-tier challenges.

2. Understand the News Trading Window
FundedPrime restricts trades within 10 minutes of major economic events (NFP, central bank decisions, etc.). This means you can't scalp the opening 10-minute volatility on high-impact news. Plan your schedule around this—focus on inter-session trading or avoid news days entirely if your strategy depends on news spikes.

3. Budget for the $50 Payout Fee Upfront
Bank transfer withdrawals carry a flat $50 fee. If you're planning to withdraw $500 in profits, that's 10% gone to fees. Either accumulate larger balances before withdrawing (reducing per-withdrawal costs) or consider cryptocurrency payouts, which may have lower or no fees depending on your setup.

4. Max Out the 2-Phase Challenge's Daily Limit Advantage
In Phase 1, the 5% daily loss cap is harsh—one bad trade ends your day. Use this to your advantage: trade smaller, focus on high-probability setups, and finish the day green early if possible. Once you hit Phase 2, the removal of the daily limit is a psychological relief that lets aggressive traders expand position sizes.

5. Leverage the 800+ Instruments Without Overcomplicating
FundedPrime's Eightcap integration gives you access to 800 instruments, but trading too many markets dilutes your edge. Pick 2–4 primary instruments (e.g., EUR/USD, GBP/USD, and Gold) and master them. Diversify only after proving consistent profitability on your core setups.

6. Export and Analyze Your Performance Reports Weekly
Download your P&L and analytics weekly. Identify which trading sessions, instruments, or times of day are most profitable. FundedPrime's dashboard provides the data—your job is to extract patterns and double down on what works.

7. Treat the 80% Profit Split as Non-Negotiable Income
Unlike some prop firms offering 70% or 50% splits, FundedPrime's 80/20 split is generous. When evaluating trades, remember that only 80% of your profits go to you. Size positions accordingly to hit your dollar targets while respecting drawdown limits.

Common FundedPrime Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Account Gets Disabled Mid-Challenge Due to Drawdown
If you hit your maximum drawdown limit, FundedPrime automatically disqualifies you. This isn't a bug—it's by design. The fix: practice strict position sizing before entering a challenge. Risk no more than 0.5–1% per trade, and you'll avoid blowing drawdown on a single bad trade. Review your trade log to identify overlevered positions.

Issue 2: Slow Withdrawal Processing
Bank transfers can take 5–10 business days to clear. If you're waiting for withdrawal confirmation, check your bank's incoming transfer status—delays are often on the bank's end, not FundedPrime's. Cryptocurrency payouts are faster (1–2 hours) if you have a wallet set up.

Issue 3: Getting Flagged for News Trading**
Accidentally traded within 10 minutes of a news event and got flagged? The platform warns you before disqualifying, so check the alert log. Next time, set calendar reminders for high-impact news times and avoid trading 15 minutes before and after scheduled announcements. FundedPrime provides an economic calendar in the dashboard—use it.

Issue 4: Confusion Between Challenge Phases
New traders often don't understand when Phase 1 ends and Phase 2 begins. FundedPrime sends an email notification, and your dashboard highlights phase completion. Once you hit the Phase 1 profit target, you don't automatically move to Phase 2—you must confirm it in-app. This prevents accidental advancement if you're not ready for the looser rules of Phase 2.

Is FundedPrime Worth It? Our Verdict

FundedPrime delivers genuine value if you're a crypto trader, a budget-conscious trader, or someone who wants flexible challenge structures without crushing time pressure. The Meme Coin challenge is genuinely unique—no competitor offers it. The 80% profit split and no-time-limit evaluations are competitive advantages. However, the non-refundable fees ($35–$67) and $50 payout penalty make every challenge a real financial commitment. Compare FundedPrime against Run Gum and MEG if you prioritize lower payouts fees, or NeuroGum if you want stricter drawdown enforcement. Start with the $35 Stock Challenge, prove profitability, and scale up. FundedPrime is worth it for disciplined traders with a working strategy—not for gamblers testing their luck on volatility.

For a detailed breakdown of how FundedPrime compares to competitors, read our full FundedPrime review. Also check out FundedPrime vs Run Gum and FundedPrime vs MEG to see head-to-head feature comparisons.
